human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for cannabis-dispensary-specific boundary discipline, patient and caregiver prerequisites, pricing and MMAP controls, complaint and recall escalation behavior, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to cannabis-dispensary patient-service intake, patient or caregiver verification support, order and pricing communication, MMAP handling, complaint intake, and evidence-backed routing tied to patient, certification, product, pricing, lot, and recall records.

Authority sources cover Pennsylvania medical-marijuana program guidance, dispensary regulations, patient and caregiver workflows, MMAP dispensary workflows, and ASTM complaint and packaging standards tied directly to cannabis retail patient-service work.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported quantity and promotion exceptions, licensed-medical handoff, adverse-event routing, repeat failures, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/cannabis-and-controlled-substance-operations/retail-patient-services-specialist/ so retail patient services stays separate from clinical consultation, compliance interpretation, pricing approval, recall disposition, and security decision authority.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around certification limits, clinical consultation, MMAP and pricing exceptions, complaint and recall handling, and unsupported delivery or refund requests.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated retail patient services specialist that supports evidence-backed patient-service handling and policy-aware escalation for regulated cannabis dispensary operators.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
